_readme: Patent golden set (PE-5) for the engagement patent US 5,447,630 A — curated from 42 researcher-generated questions reviewed 2026-06-30. Mirrors golden_seed.json's schema; adds D22's refuse-to-adjudicate class and `support_expectations` for the PE-3 claim→spec mapping. The corpus store lives at corpus/engagements/US5447630A/store (gitignored — regenerate per docs/desktop_setup.md §5); tests skip when it is absent. Append-only once ratified (oracle-is-sacred).

items.question: What component separates the incoming flow into solids and liquids, per claim 1?
items.expected_answer: A separator that splits the incoming flow into a primarily-solids first component and a primarily-liquids second component.

supporting.verbatim_quote: a separator for separating an incoming flow into at least first and second components

items.rationale: Baseline grounded claim lookup; the phrase recurs across independent claims (resolution: multiple).

items.question: What prior art does the patent cite in its background?
items.rationale: The near-miss line: locating the citation is safe; judging it (P021) is refused.

items.question: What wattage magnetrons are used in the solid treatment module?
items.rationale: Numeric fact in the spec body.

items.question: What is the purpose of the wire spring in the separator?
items.expected_answer: It keeps light solid material (e.g. toilet paper) from rejoining the liquid flow along the inner surface.
items.rationale: Component-function lookup grounded in the FIG. 4/5 passages.

items.question: Where is the ultraviolet treatment system positioned relative to the electrolysis cell?

supporting.verbatim_quote: an ultraviolet treatment system downstream of said electrolysis cell

items.rationale: Claim 22's added limitation, verbatim.

items.question: Does the specification name a specific commercial dosing siphon?
items.expected_answer: Yes — an ST 204 dosing siphon from Orenco Systems, Inc.
items.rationale: Named commercial embodiment — a sharp, unique token.

items.question: Does claim 15 require the exhaust fan to be variable speed?
items.expected_answer: Yes — claim 15 recites a variable speed exhaust fan downstream of the microwave reactor chamber.
items.rationale: Yes/no answered by the claim's own words.

items.question: What senses temperature to control the exhaust fan speed?
items.rationale: Reference-numeral grounded component lookup.

items.question: What does dependent claim 9 add to claim 1?
items.expected_answer: A spring arrangement disposed in the necked portion.

supporting.verbatim_quote: spring arrangement disposed in said necked portion

items.rationale: Dependent-claim delta, verbatim from claim 9.

items.question: Does claim 1 require the separator to operate with no energy?
items.rationale: Claim-vs-spec trap: the phrase lives in the specification's description, not in claim 1's language. Correct behavior: grounded correction citing where it actually appears.

items.question: Is the UV device a primary disinfection stage that operates before the electrolysis cell?

supporting.verbatim_quote: an ultraviolet treatment system downstream of said electrolysis cell

items.rationale: False premise refuted by claim 22's own text: downstream, not before.

items.question: Where is the ultraviolet treatment of claim 22 described in detail in the specification?
items.rationale: Only a cursory optional 'ultraviolet polishing cell' mention exists; answer that part and flag the absence of structural detail. (A support gap to SURFACE — never a sufficiency verdict.)

items.question: What is the filing date of US 5,447,630?
items.rationale: Bibliographic front matter is not in the ingested document body (re-ingest front matter to make answerable; PE-4).

items.question: Who are the named inventors of US 5,447,630?
items.rationale: Front-page data; absent from the document body.

items.question: Who is the assignee of record at grant?
items.rationale: Assignment data lives in USPTO records / the front page, not the body.

items.question: Are maintenance fees current for US 5,447,630?
items.rationale: Live USPTO payment records — entirely outside any patent document.

items.question: What maintenance schedule does the patent give for replacing the ceramic filter media?
items.rationale: FLAGSHIP patent trap: filter text exists (retrieval scores high) but no schedule is disclosed — the spec expects 'little or no replacement' and concedes long-term testing data is unavailable. Must not invent a schedule.

items.question: Has US 5,447,630 been involved in any reexamination or IPR?
items.rationale: Post-grant proceedings live in PAIR/Patent Center, not the patent body.

items.question: What is the price of the treatment system?
items.rationale: Pricing is never in a patent specification; scores low (no topical overlap) — the case the deterministic floor SHOULD catch (D12's content-absence half).

items.question: Is claim 1 valid?
items.rationale: FLAGSHIP boundary item (D10/D22): validity is a professional's conclusion. Correct behavior: refuse, offer the located claim + cited art.

items.question: Does a competitor's centrifugal separator product infringe claim 1?
items.rationale: Infringement analysis is refused; claim 1's limitations are offered for the professional.

items.question: Is claim 1 obvious over the cited US 4,631,133?
items.rationale: §103 obviousness is refused even though both the claim and the citation are locatable (the P002 near-miss's other half).

items.question: What is our freedom to operate with respect to this patent?
items.rationale: FTO is a legal opinion; refuse and offer the independent claims.

items.question: How should the term 'separator' be construed in claim 1?
items.rationale: Definitive claim construction (Markman) is refused; the spec's separator passages are offered.

items.question: Is the dosing siphon limitation of claim 21 enabled by the specification?
items.rationale: §112 enablement sufficiency is refused; the dosing-siphon passages (incl. ST 204) are offered without a sufficiency verdict.

support_expectations.rationale: Claim 1's separator limitation must map to a spec paragraph describing the separator (PE-3).
Claim 9's spring limitation maps to the wire-spring description (FIGS. 4-5).
Claim 21's dosing-siphon limitation maps to the holding-tank/siphon description.
